paycard-amount.description: Amount in GNF. Must be greater than 0.

paycard-operation-reference.description: Your own reference for this transaction. If omitted, Paycard generates one.

paycard-callback-url.description: URL Paycard will POST to when the payment completes.

paycard-auto-redirect.description: Whether to redirect the user automatically after payment.

paycard-redirect-with-get.description: Append paycard-operation-reference as a query param on redirect.

paycard-jump-to-paycard.description: Skip payment method selection and go directly to Paycard wallet.

paycard-jump-to-cc.description: Skip payment method selection and go directly to credit card.

paycard-jump-to-om.description: Skip payment method selection and go directly to Orange Money.

paycard-jump-to-momo.description: Skip payment method selection and go directly to MTN MoMo.

paycard-payment-url.description: URL to redirect the user to complete the payment.

paycard-operation-reference.description: Transaction reference. Store this to verify the payment later.

paycard-amount.description: Amount echoed back as a string.

gotchas: Always verify payment status server-side by calling verify_payment before fulfilling an order. The callback URL alone is not sufficient — it can be spoofed by anyone who knows your endpoint.
Store paycard-operation-reference from the response immediately. It is the only way to verify the payment status later. If you passed your own reference, use that.
Auth is the field 'c' sent in the POST body, not an HTTP header. Do not confuse it with a Bearer token or X-Api-Key pattern.
There is no sandbox environment. All requests hit production. Use minimal amounts (e.g. 100 GNF) when testing.
